summary: Epic Dark Star, raging Eleven‑Caution and tight jams all night
blurb: The set bursts with a soaring Turn On Your Lovelight into an expansive Dark Star, then rolls through Saint Stephen into The Eleven and a ferocious Caution climax. Jerry’s guitar is hypnotic and Phil’s bass thunderous throughout, with Pigpen adding depth on the organ. Highlights include the magical Lovelight segue and relentless energy from start to finish.
review:
On October 20, 1968 at Berkeley’s Greek Theatre the Dead delivered a blistering one‑set performance that still feels fresh decades later. Opening with Good Morning Little School Girl, the band quickly escalated into a searing Turn On Your Lovelight that melted into an otherworldly Dark Star, where Jerry Garcia’s tone was described as “hypnotic” and “amazing”. The momentum never faltered – Saint Stephen segued straight into The Eleven, which exploded into a thunderous Caution (Do Not Stop On Tracks) finale capped by a wild Feedback climax. Pigpen’s organ presence on the jams added unexpected depth, while Phil Lesh anchored the chaos with a booming, Jack Casady‑style bass that shone especially on School Girl. Throughout, the group stayed relentlessly high‑energy, delivering what reviewers call an “epic” and “magical” set despite the brief runtime.

Both Miller soundboard tapes capture this night in crystal‑clear fidelity; the 21441 recording is noted as excellent with only a faint buzz in Lovelight, while the 9071 tape is praised for its clean mix despite minor level quirks. The superior board source (gd68-10-20.sbd.miller.21441.sbeok.shnf) offers the most complete and sonically pristine listening experience, making it the definitive version for fans seeking to relive this historic set.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings are uniformly positive, repeatedly praising Dark Star, The Eleven, Lovelight and the Eleven‑Caution climax. Multiple reviewers call the playing "amazing", "hypnotic" and note a "relentless" energy throughout, indicating strong consensus of exceptional performance.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard fidelity with crystal‑clear mix and full set captured; minor issues are negligible compared to its superb overall quality.

key_highlights: Epic Dark Star segue from Lovelight, showcasing Garcia’s hypnotic tone.
Raging Eleven into a ferocious Caution climax, highlighted as legendary.
Magical Turn On Your Lovelight performance with Pigpen’s organ depth.
Tight band cohesion and relentless energy throughout the one‑set show.

band_performance.Jerry: Shows a hypnotic tone on Dark Star, fiery fills on Lovelight and powerful leads on The Eleven, with solid playing throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Provides booming, Jack Casady‑style bass, especially prominent on Good Morning Little School Girl and extended solo during the set.
